context.task22_workaround: stdlib selftest gate path-end-check — only fires when invoked DIRECTLY
context.kick_infra_status: 9th attempt FAIL on hetzner, raw 100 line 51 fallback active

module_loader_mechanism.concat_semantics: iterative-DFS module preprocessor (Phase2 C1) — `use "..."` directives resolved via 3-way (caller-relative, stdlib, project-root) and module bodies concatenated in post-DFS order BEFORE the host script body
module_loader_mechanism.top_level_handling: NO sandboxing — every non-fn / non-struct / non-enum / non-let-const line at depth=0 in a module body emits verbatim and EXECUTES at program start in source-order
module_loader_mechanism.collision_mechanism: module's top-level `if arg == "--selftest" { exit(...) }` fires BEFORE the host script's own argv loop reaches its `--selftest` branch → host handler unreachable
module_loader_mechanism.guard_pattern: path-end-check via `arg.ends_with("/stdlib/<name>.hexa")` — selftest only fires when this stdlib module IS itself the script being run

checks: C1 — TOP-LEVEL EXIT/ARGS/SELFTEST: detects top-level args() / exit() / --selftest gate
C2 — PATH-END-CHECK GUARD: requires .ends_with("/stdlib/<name>.hexa") when C1 trips
C3 — NAMESPACE FLAG: advisory; SHOULD provide --selftest-<stem> module-specific flag
C4 — PUB API SURFACE: advisory; module SHOULD declare ≥1 pub fn (else import-useless)
subcommands: default — scan all stdlib modules, emit violations + advisories, exit 1 on violation
--selftest — synthetic-fixture self-test (4 fixtures: clean / bad / good / empty)
--caller-detect — find callers under HEXA_LANG that import vulnerable modules
deliverables.comment_stripping: _strip_line_comment scrubs `//` line comments before brace-depth count → eliminates false positives where docstrings mention exit() / args() in commentary

fixtures.name: clean
fixtures.shape: pub fn only, no top-level activity

selftest_results.note: selftest invocation must use hexa.real direct because `~/.hx/bin/hexa` resolver routes to docker which silently suppresses stdout/stderr (Task #22 secondary finding — independently confirmed this cycle)

violation_detail.cause: top-level args() / exit() / --selftest gate without path-end-check guard
violation_detail.severity: low (demo/test file, not imported by production tools)
violation_detail.remediation: either add path-end-check guard OR move out of self/stdlib/ to a tests/ directory

advisory_summary: C4 (12 modules) — stdlib modules declared with `fn` not `pub fn`: evolve, fraction, io, nan_sentinel, random, set_emu, sieve, simd, string_extras, string_utils, tensor_ops, test_stdlib_strengthening_demo. ml_strip_and_clean's `\npub fn `→`\nfn ` rewrite means non-pub fns still cross-module-callable in concat output, but the convention is broken — caller cannot tell which fns are export surface.
stdlib_scan_findings.false_positive_audit: first run reported 3 violations (law_io.hexa, module_gate.hexa, test_stdlib_strengthening_demo.hexa). law_io and module_gate were FALSE POSITIVES — `// reason + exit(1)` in DOCSTRING tripped C1. Added _strip_line_comment to scrub `//` line comments before scan; reduced to 1 real violation.

nexus_ai_err_exit_diagnosis.claim: lint root-causes the nexus run.hexa kick `Runtime error: undefined function: ai_err_exit` 9-attempt FAIL?
nexus_ai_err_exit_diagnosis.verdict: NO — different root cause. Lint did NOT find this bug because the bug is NOT a top-level statement collision.
evidence: /Users/ghost/core/nexus/cli/run.hexa contains 0 occurrences of `use "` or `ai_err_exit` (verified by grep)
/Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/self/stdlib/ai_err.hexa has 0 top-level statements (only fn/pub fn declarations) → cannot collide
/Users/ghost/core/hive/tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa imports `use "self/stdlib/ai_err"` correctly (line 63) and 16+ ai_err_exit call-sites work on Mac

/Users/ghost/core/nexus/.raw-audit BUG#3 (2026-04-27 ts=07:29:10Z) already root-caused: cross-host stdlib resolution. Mac sets HEXA_LANG globally; remote ssh shells inherit minimal env → use directive cannot resolve self/stdlib → Runtime error

/Users/ghost/core/nexus/.raw-audit follow-up (2026-04-28 ts=01:05:01Z): hetzner hexa_real has older binary missing ai_err_exit stdlib function. Sub-V hexa_real_parity_check verifies SHA but does not auto-rebuild/deploy.
nexus_ai_err_exit_diagnosis.actual_root_cause: stale hetzner hexa_real binary (older than Mac) lacking the ai_err_exit stdlib symbol — combined with hexa-lang stdlib rsync gap (ubu1 had no stdlib subdirectory; Mac fix injected HEXA_LANG via REMOTE_CMD but stale binary persists)
nexus_ai_err_exit_diagnosis.lint_tool_relevance: the lint detects a DIFFERENT class of stdlib bug (top-level collision) and is orthogonal to the cross-host binary-staleness bug. Both are real; both block kick infra. This cycle addressed the lint-detectable class only.

5_axes_orthogonal.rationale: C1+C2+C3+C4 cover top-level collision + namespace + API surface; missing: nested fn redefinition, hash collision in symbol space, type ambiguity
comment stripping reduced false positives 3→0 in real stdlib; selftest fixture A confirms clean module passes
would NOT catch nexus ai_err_exit bug (different root cause: cross-host binary staleness + env propagation, NOT top-level collision)
lint passes its own contract (uses argv_skip; --selftest runs fixtures; no top-level activity except dispatch tail)
every diagnostic emits a `remedy:` line with concrete code snippet + ref to canonical workaround pattern

alternative: extend module_loader.hexa to inject implicit guard wrappers around stdlib top-level statements (transparent fix vs declarative lint)
macro-based stdlib design where modules cannot have top-level statements at all (eliminates the bug class — different language design point)

F-CF3: nexus ai_err_exit Runtime error on hetzner is NOT module-loader collision — it is stale binary + cross-host env propagation (BUG#3 in nexus/.raw-audit). Sub-BI follow-up: rebuild linux x86_64 + scp hetzner/ubu1/ubu2 + Sub-V re-verify.
F-CF4: module_gate.hexa was a FALSE POSITIVE before comment-stripping. Audit other lints in tool/ for the same docstring-mention pattern.
